_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-2 Child Abuse and Neglect (Revised 5/23/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
condition: Child Abuse and Neglect — Mandatory Reporting
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-2: Child Abuse and Neglect — MDHHS Approved 5/23/2023; MCL 722.623
purpose: EMS providers are mandatory reporters under MCL 722.623. All EMS personnel who have reasonable cause to suspect child abuse or neglect MUST report directly to the Department of Health and Human Services (DHHS). Reporting to a supervisor or organization does NOT fulfill the individual's mandatory reporting obligation.

sections.title: Legal Obligations
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 7-2: Child Abuse — Legal Obligations (MCL 722.623)
points: MCL 722.623: ALL EMS personnel are mandatory reporters of suspected child abuse or neglect
Reporting requirement applies even if the patient is NOT transported
Call 855-444-3911 IMMEDIATELY to report suspected abuse or neglect
Written DHS-3200 form must be completed within 72 hours of the initial verbal report
Notifying the EMS organization head does NOT fulfill the individual mandatory reporting requirement — each provider must report directly
Do NOT confront or accuse suspected abusers at the scene

sections.title: Indicators of Possible Child Abuse or Neglect
points: Injury inconsistent with stated mechanism of injury or developmental stage of the child
Scald burns with clear immersion lines (glove, stocking, or doughnut pattern)
Cigarette or small circular burns
Bruising in non-ambulatory child (infant/young child who cannot crawl or walk)
Pregnancy in child under 12 years of age
Pattern injuries suggesting use of objects (belt, cord, hand)
Fractures inconsistent with stated mechanism or developmental stage
Multiple injuries in various stages of healing
Delayed presentation for medical care
Caregiver providing inconsistent or changing histories
Indicators of neglect: malnourishment, unsafe living environment, lack of appropriate clothing or supervision, medical neglect

sections.title: Scene and Documentation Guidance
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 7-2: Child Abuse — Scene and Documentation
points: Primary duty is emergency medical care — treat and stabilize per appropriate protocol
Do NOT confront or accuse the suspected abuser at the scene
Document verbatim any excited utterances made by the child or caregiver — use quotation marks
Document the child's demeanor, affect, and behavior
Document the physical environment if relevant to suspicion of neglect
Document injuries with precise descriptions (location, size, color, shape, pattern)
Document caregiver(s) present and their relationship to the child
Do NOT delay transport to investigate or document
If patient is not transported: mandatory report is still required
Attach DHS-3200 written report within 72 hours of verbal report

deterioration_flags: Life-threatening injuries — stabilize and transport per appropriate treatment protocol; mandatory report still required
Child in immediate danger at scene — coordinate with law enforcement for scene safety while maintaining patient care
Caregiver interfering with care — involve law enforcement; prioritize patient care
scope_notes: ALL EMS provider levels are mandatory reporters under MCL 722.623
Mandatory report hotline: 855-444-3911 — call immediately upon suspicion
Written DHS-3200 form required within 72 hours of verbal report
Reporting to supervisor alone does NOT fulfill legal obligation
Report is required even when patient is not transported
Do not confront suspected abusers — that is a law enforcement function
